title: AI-Driven Multi-Document Correlation for Financial Compliance - Varsha Shah, Independent
summary: An AI-driven framework combines graph-based entity correlation, adaptive probabilistic risk modeling, and cross-jurisdictional normalization to detect fraud patterns across multiple financial documents in isolation. The approach evaluated three million anonymized records across four jurisdictions and demonstrates improved fraud detection accuracy with reduced false positives and manual audit effort.
writeup: This talk presents an AI framework for detecting financial compliance risks across multiple enterprise documents, combining graph-based entity correlation, adaptive probabilistic risk modeling, and cross-jurisdictional normalization. The framework was evaluated on three million anonymized financial records spanning four jurisdictions. The reported results: reduced false positives and lower manual audit effort in fraud detection. The cross-jurisdictional normalization component addresses the problem of reconciling compliance signals across differing regulatory regimes.
